name: history-workflow
description: Histories sample a single variable at a common interval during cycling. They are used for convergence checks, load/displacement curves, pore-pressure response, dynamic traces, and other validation outputs.

notes: All histories use one global sampling interval.
A smaller interval gives finer curves but increases stored data.
Use FLAC2D coordinates for FLAC2D and FLAC3D coordinates for FLAC3D.
Only one monitored variable is created per history command.
Use history names or IDs consistently in exports.
Use tables when the next step stays inside FLAC; use files for external plotting.

management_keywords.description: Set the global sampling interval in cycles or steps.
Print a summary of configured histories.
Erase stored history values while keeping history definitions.
Write selected histories to screen, file, or table.

validation_notes: Name histories so exported curves remain self-describing.
For staged analysis, export or purge histories at stage boundaries when the baseline changes.
Use product-specific coordinates: avoid z-position probes in FLAC2D commands.
